rulings.Case Title: FLORENCE KONG VS. MARIANO HERNANDEZ ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: HEARING RE: ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE WHY SANCTIONS SHOULD NOT BE IMPOSED/CONTEMPT
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 28, 2024 line 1. HEARING RE: ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE WHY SANCTIONS SHOULD NOT BE IMPOSED/CONTEMPT Hearing Required. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: ALEJANDRO CUEVAS VS. DOUGLAS H. HERNANDEZ ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, UNLAWFUL DETAINER
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 28, 2024 line 7. PLAINTIFF ALEJANDRO CUEVAS MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, UNLAWFUL DETAINER is DENIED. Moving party failed to shift its burden to show that there are no triable issues of fact and that Plaintiff is entitled to a judgment as a matter of law. Specifically, there are no authorities cited and no evidence provided that Plaintiff is "the owner" within the meaning of TPOA 2019; a "landlord" within the meaning of SFRO and a master tenant exempt from just cause eviction requirement. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.
